title: Continuous micro mixer
abstract: A multimodal micromixer obstacle for intensification of mixing and performing the reaction in a continuous manner is disclosed herein. The micromixer 100 comprises of plurality of inlets, an outlet and a plurality of channels. The end channels- of the channels, have plurality- of converging sections having width, to depth ratio ranging 1:1 to 20:1. The intermediate channels have at least, one obstacle having non-circular shape. Each converging section is incomplete ellipse, prolate or oblate shaped having, angle of curvature in the range of 90 to 270Â°. Axes of the inlets are coplanar and perpendicular to the channels. All the components of the micromixer are coplanar.
claims: Claims We Claim, 1. A multimodal micromixer comprising plurality of inlets [202-204], an outlet [205] and a plurality of channels wherein end channels of said plurality of channels comprise plurality of converging sections [702] having width to depth ratio ranging 1: 1 to 20: 1, and intermediate channels having at least one obstacle [703] for intensification of mixing and performing the reaction in a continuous manner. 2. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1 , wherein each converging section [702] is incomplete ellipse [701A-C] , prolate [70 ID] or oblate [70 IE] shaped having angle of curvature in the range of 90 to 270Â°. 3. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 2, wherein ratio of radii of curvature of incomplete ellipse, prolate or oblate shaped is in the range of 0.1: 10. 4. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1, wherein said obstacle [703] in said intermediate channel have non-circular shape selected from triangular, rectangular or any such shape. 5. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1, wherein said obstacles as well as said converging sections are arranged in periodic or aperiodic sequence. 6. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1, wherein intermediate channels having said obstacles have sharp corners. 7. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1, wherein axis of said inlets are co- planar and perpendicular to said channels. 8. The multimodal micromixer according to any of the claims, wherein said converging sections [702], said obstacles [703], said inlets [202-204] and said outlet [205] are co- planar. 9. The multimodal micromixer according to claim 1, wherein said plurality of channels have serpentine nature. 10. A multimodal micromixer comprising a plurality of channels and intermediate channels, wherein end channels of said plurality of channels comprise plurality of converging sections [702] having width to depth ratio ranging 1: 1 to 20: 1, said converging sections [702] are incomplete ellipse [701A-C] , prolate [70 ID] or oblate [70 IE] shaped having angle of curvature in the range of 90 to 270Â°; ratio of radii of curvature of incomplete ellipse, prolate or oblate shaped is in the range of 0.1: 10; said intermediate channels having at least one obstacle [703] for intensification of mixing and performing the reaction in a continuous manner; said obstacle [703] in said intermediate channel have non-circular shape selected from triangular, rectangular or any such shape, plurality of inlets [202-204] being co-planar and perpendicular to said channels; and an outlet [205], wherein said converging sections [702], said obstacles [703], said inlets [202-204] and said outlet [205] are co-planar.
